title: Great Expectations
author: Dickens, Charles
summary: Orphaned boy Pip narrates his youth in a bleak marsh country beside the river and sea. While mourning at family tombstones he is accosted by a fearsome escaped convict who forces him to promise to bring food and a file, threatening death. Terrified, Pip returns to his household: stern sister Mrs. Joe Gargery, her mild blacksmith husband Joe, and the forge. Mrs. Joe rules with a cane called Tickler. Pip’s childish imaginings and graveyard impressions shape his understanding of family, danger, and obligation, setting a tone of fear, dependency, and strange encounters that define his early life and memory too.
